title: Data Leakage Prevention in Feature Stores
author: Databricks Workshop — Bangalore

slides.title: What we'll cover
items: What data leakage is and why it hurts

slides.text: Data leakage: using feature values for training that were not available at the time the label was recorded.

slides.title: A 2-minute mistake
slides.card_content:
The trap

Leakage makes offline
scores look excellent.

You only notice when
production accuracy
collapses.

slides.text: For each labeled event at time t, join the feature value that was current at or before t — never after.

slides.title: AS OF join — the mechanic
slides.content:
For each label row, the Feature Store:
  1. matches by primary key (excluding timestamp)
  2. takes the LATEST feature timestamp <= the label timestamp
  3. returns null if no prior value exists

User 1 labeled 08:50  ->  reading from 08:45  (NOT 08:52)

slides.title: Declare the timeseries column
slides.content:
CREATE TABLE main.fs.user_reading_features (
  user_id       STRING    NOT NULL,
  feature_ts    TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
  reading_value DOUBLE,
  CONSTRAINT pk PRIMARY KEY (user_id, feature_ts TIMESERIES)
);

# Python: fe.create_table(..., timeseries_columns='feature_ts')

slides.title: Point-in-time training set
bullets: timestamp_lookup_key triggers the AS OF join
Matches feature value known at/before the label
Returns null when no prior value exists

slides.title: Bounding history — lookback_window
left: Excludes features older than a cutoff

slides.text: score_batch re-applies the same point-in-time logic at inference — leak-free training AND skew-free serving.

items.text: Use time series tables + timestamp_lookup_key for time-varying features
Split before engineering; don't fit scalers on full data
Never include the target or a proxy as a feature
Respect label windows (don't use in-window features)
Be suspicious of 'too good' offline metrics
Use lookback_window to drop stale features

slides.title: Key takeaways
bullets: Leakage = training on info unavailable at label time
Point-in-time: use the value known at or before t
Time series tables enable AS OF joins
timestamp_lookup_key makes the training set leak-free
score_batch re-applies the same logic — no skew
